name: Why We Taste With Our Ears
description: This lecture promises to be an evening that rethinks something as simple as eating, by tuning to a sense we rarely notice at the table.About the LectureWhy does the sound of rain make fried food more irresistible? Why does music change how we experience a glass of wine?This session begins with a simple but surprising idea: taste doesn’t start in the mouth, it often begins in the ear.Drawing on extensive research by Bruite Magazine across the subcontinent, this lecture explores how sound quietly shapes the way we anticipate, experience, and remember food. From the rhythm of a bustling street to the curated playlists of a fine-dining restaurant, auditory cues influence everything from perceived texture to indulgence and value.Moving across everyday eating environments and contemporary food spaces, the session explores:The Sound of Anticipation: How auditory cues shape cravings even before the first biteSonic Texture: Why crunch, sizzle, and silence can alter how we perceive flavour and freshnessAtmosphere & Memory: How music, noise, and rhythm influence mood and the way meals stay with usDesigning Food Experiences: The role of sound in restaurants, bars, supper clubs, and immersive dining formatsBlending cultural observation, field research, and sharp storytelling, this session offers a fresh lens on something deeply familiar.Flow of the Evening5:00 PM: Doors open at Siyah Arthouse, Saket: Come in, find your seat, and get comfortable5:00 – 5:20 PM: Place your orders and start a few conversations5:30 PM: The learning begins6:15 PM: Open Q&A and discussion with Ms. Ramya6:30 PM onwards: Stay a while: More eating, more drinking, more conversationsAbout the SpeakerRamya G Sri is the Co-founder and Editor-in-Chief of Bruite Magazine, one of South Asia’s leading independent food and culture publications known for its deeply researched, original storytelling and on-ground reporting.Through Bruite, she collaborates with chefs, historians, artisans, and communities across the subcontinent to explore how food intersects with culture, memory, and perception—often through unexpected and underexplored lenses.She also co-leads Bruite Media Co., a specialised brand advisory that has worked with over 500 brands and founders across F&B, lifestyle, D2C, and consumer tech. Her work spans brand launches, communication strategy, and creative training—shaping how brands tell stories and connect with audiences.Her contributions to storytelling have been recognised with the Laadli Media Award for Gender-Sensitive Storytelling and the Abby Silver Award for Advertising. Across her work, she brings together rigorous research, editorial depth, and a strong instinct for how narrative shapes both culture and consumer experience.About unLectureunLecture brings experts out of classrooms and into cafés, bars and neighbourhood spaces across Delhi. Created by three friends from college who wanted learning to feel warm, social and alive. unLecture turns an otherwise ordinary evening into a conversation you may think about long after it ends.

name: Sham-e-Qutub : A night heritage walk
description: We are delighted to invite you to a special *Night heritage walk at Qutub Minar*, curated by Darwesh Heritage Walks**Date: 30th May, 2026**Time: 7:00 p.m. onwardsExperience the magic of one of Delhi’s most iconic monuments as it transforms at dusk—when the Qutub Minar complex is beautifully lit and wrapped in an almost timeless charm.This walk takes you through the story of Delhi’s first capital at Mehrauli, tracing the rise of the Tomars and Chauhans, and the arrival of the Delhi Sultanate under the early Turko-Afghan rulers. As the evening sets in, the illuminated ruins, soaring minar, and intricate carvings create a truly immersive atmosphere—perfect for experiencing history not just as a narrative, but as a living, breathing space.During this walk, we will explore:* The origins of Delhi’s earliest city at Mehrauli* The political transitions from Rajput to Sultanate rule* The architectural brilliance of the Qutub complex and its symbolism* The stories hidden in its mosques, pillars, and inscriptionsJoin us for an unforgettable evening of storytelling, history, and exploration—where the past comes alive under the soft glow of lights.We look forward to having you with us.(Please note: the fee is not for access to the site, but rather for the curated experience)

name: Unscript 2.0: Improv Comedy Show
description: After 14 hours of high-energy, no-holds-barred workshopping, this ensemble—now working at Level 2—steps into performance with sharper instincts, bolder choices, and a deepened sense of play. What unfolds is completely unscripted, wildly unpredictable, and built in the momenThis isn’t just a show you watch. It’s one you fuel.Your suggestions spark scenes. Your ideas twist the story. Your laughter drives the rhythm.A single word can spiral into a love story, a disaster, or something beautifully absurd. One performer holds the thread, the rest run with it—and somehow, chaos turns into magic.No scripts. No second takes. Just quick thinking, deep listening, and fearless fun.Expect moments that land perfectly, moments that go hilariously sideways, and everything in between. That’s where the real joy lives.HighlightsFully improvised, unscripted comedyAudience participation shapes the showPerformed by Level 2 trained ensembleHigh-energy, interactive experienceEvery show is completely uniqueAbout the HostAnshu DagaAn improv facilitator and performer guiding immersive, high-energy workshops that focus on spontaneity, presence, and creative expression.Come for the spontaneity. Stay for the surprises.Don’t plan the night—just secure your seat!

name: Safdarjung Tomb : Heritage Walk
description: Safdarjung’s Tomb Heritage Walk(curated by Darwesh Heritage Walks in collaboration with Sabhyata Foundation)Join us for a journey into the fading glow of Mughal splendour at Safdarjung's Tomb — often called the *last flicker of Mughal grandeur* in Delhi.Built in 1754 for Safdarjung, the powerful Nawab of Awadh and Prime Minister of the Mughal court, this monument stands at the twilight of an empire — grand in ambition, yet carrying within it the quiet signs of decline.Its story is as fascinating as its form. From courtly intrigues and shifting loyalties to the architectural choices that reveal a changing political landscape, Safdarjung’s life opens a compelling window into 18th-century Delhi.We will also trace a lesser-known layer of its history — how this tomb was constructed by reusing material from a pre-existing Mughal structure, quite literally building one memory over another.To truly understand Delhi — its rise, its resilience, and its gradual transitions — a visit to Safdarjung’s Tomb is essential.Come, walk with us through the final chapter of Mughal architectural brilliance — where grandeur meets gravity, and history lingers in every arch.Plz note: (fee charged is not for entry into the site , but for the curated experience)

name: Walk through Excavations & findings at Purana Qila
description: Q. Ever wondered why archeologists keep digging up Purana Qila??Q. Do you also think Purana Qila is just a Mughal fort??Q. Did you know? Digging just a few meters into the soil at Purana Qila has a direct bearing on the mystery of Indraprastha, the Mahabharata, and Delhi's oldest secrets??What to expect from the Walk:If you are curious about the questions above, expect a really interesting, thought provoking discussion that you don't usually hear in regular conversations about Delhi. We will look at the major findings from the recent digs and talk about the actual debates surrounding the Indraprastha connection. It is also a classic example of how politics enters history—or how history enters politics. If you want to see the capital’s roots through a completely new lens, this walk is for you.Quick Heads-Up: Location: Inside the museum (Not outside / not the Purana Qila outskirts).Language: English+HindiParking & Lockers: Subject strictly to the venue's existing arrangements. Organizers do not guarantee or assure availability, so please travel light.Arrival: Please arrive 10 minutes early at the main entrance to clear security smoothly.

name: Overthinkers Anonymous
description: OverviewEver wondered why you default to people pleasing, perfectionism, or avoidance? Overthinkers Anonymous is a small and guided space to slow down, reflect, and talk honestly about the things that occupy our minds. This session focuses on Family Patterns and how the environments we grew up in still shape how we think, feel, and respond today.We focus on connecting the dots between past experiences and present reactions in a safe and judgment free environment. The goal is to build awareness and self compassion rather than focusing on blame.What is Stories of Hope?We are a community-led initiative dedicated to creating safe, mindful spaces. Through workshops and gatherings, we help individuals navigate life’s complexities, from mental well-being to personal growth, with empathy and shared human connection.What to expectPattern Recognition: Explore why tendencies like overthinking or control keep showing up in your life.Guided Reflection: Use a specialized worksheet to gain context on your reactions without the weight of self-judgment.Awareness & Compassion: A gentle space to build self-awareness and understanding—focused on insight, not on reliving trauma.Details🗓️ Date: 31st May (Sunday)⏰ Time: 11:30 AM📍 Location: Kanchanjunga Towers🎟️ Contribution: ₹700 (Includes Coffee + Snacks)

name: Wood Working Workshop- Make & Take
description: First of it's kind in Delhi-NCRStep into our workshop and experience the joy of building something with your own hands. Woodling Wooden Workshops are designed to bring people together through creativity, craftsmanship, and meaningful experiences.Our Make & Take Workshop is a hands-on experience where you get to design, craft, and take home your very own wooden creation. Guided by experts from a legacy timber background, you’ll work with real tools and high-quality wood in a safe, beginner-friendly environment. Beyond just creating something tangible, woodworking offers a deeply calming and rewarding experience. It helps improve focus, boosts creativity, reduces stress, and gives a sense of accomplishment that’s hard to match. Whether you’re looking for a mindful break, a fun activity, or a new hobby, this workshop lets you slow down, work with your hands, and reconnect with the joy of making something from scratch.We are pioneers in the timber industry, operating since 1991. Based in Faridabad—at the heart of the timber market—we are proud to be one of the largest and most established names in the region.What you can make in our 3 hour workshop-Hand-carved wooden spoonTea light candle holderWooden coasters (set of 2)Mini desk organiserPhoto frameKey holder / wall hooksSmall trinket traySmall Book shelfHand carvingAnd many many more!Whether you join us with friends, as a couple, with your team, or even solo, you will leave with a product you made yourself and a deeper appreciation for the craft behind it.Perfect for creative weekends, Birthday parties, group outings, team bonding, or simply trying something new.Come build. Create. Take home more than just a product. 🪵✨

description: One table. Ten seats. And a journey straight back to the slow, golden flavors of your childhood. This is not a traditional dinner; it’s an intimate, gathering-style dining experience designed around connection, storytelling, and culinary time travel. We invite you to pull up a chair, lean into the music, and share a single table with a small group of fellow food lovers as we bring summer nostalgia completely reimagined to the plate.Over the course of the afternoon, the boundaries between strangers disappear. As the plates roll out from the kitchen, you’ll experience an unhurried, multi-course menu where childhood food memories, from retro canteen favorites to the comfort of your family kitchen, are elevated into premium, artisanal dishes. Come ready to share a seat, engage in great conversation, and lose yourself in a beautifully curated food experience built entirely around the magic of endless summers.Event DetailsDates: Saturday & Sunday, May 30th & 31stTable Size: Strictly capped at 10 guests per session for an intimate experience.

name: Trauma Porn: The Pain We Are Trained To Recognise
description: Join us on Sunday, May 31, for an evening that questions the stories we are drawn to  and what it means when suffering becomes something we consume.About the LectureStorytelling has never been neutral. But what happens when pain becomes the most visible, shareable, and compelling form of narrative?This session explores the growing phenomenon often described as ‘trauma porn’, where suffering is shaped, framed, and circulated in ways that make it legible, gripping, and consumable. In an attention economy driven by intensity and immediacy, trauma can begin to function as narrative currency, promoting dramatic and emotionally charged stories over stories that are quieter, slower, or harder to define.Drawing from trauma theory and cultural analysis, this lecture examines how these patterns extend beyond media and into the way we understand our own lives.Flow of the Evening 4:00 PM: Doors open at Depot 48, GK II: Come in, find your seat, and get comfortable4:05 – 4:20 PM: Place your orders and start a few conversations4:30 PM: The learning begins5:15 PM: Open Q&A and discussion with Ms. Gupta5:30 PM onwards: Stay a while: More eating, more drinking, more conversationsAbout the SpeakerVasundhara Gupta is a trauma-focused psychotherapist, narrative practitioner, and the Founder of Humraahi Psychotherapy, a therapist-led mental health practice based in New Delhi. Her work explores the intersection of trauma, storytelling, and the politics of recognition, examining how the narratives available to us shape what we are able to feel, articulate, and survive.Trained at Lady Shri Ram College for Women, University of Delhi, and the Tata Institute of Social Sciences, her therapeutic practice is rooted in relational and process-oriented care, with a particular focus on trauma, grief, identity, and relational complexity.As a narrative practitioner, she is deeply interested in the dominant stories that organise our inner lives, and in what becomes possible when those stories are questioned, re-authored, and collectively re-imagined. Alongside her clinical work, she creates reflective spaces for therapists and peer supporters, and has facilitated workshops, trainings, and support groups across academic and community settings.At the centre of her work is a question she continually returns to: how do we learn to listen to suffering, our own and others’, with greater care, complexity, and responsibility.About unLectureunLecture brings experts out of classrooms and into cafés, bars and neighbourhood spaces across Delhi. Created by three friends from St. Stephen’s, Delhi who wanted learning to feel warm, social and alive. unLecture turns an otherwise ordinary evening into a conversation you may think about long after it ends.

location.address: M9, Level, block market, 2, M, Block M, Greater Kailash II, Greater Kailash, New Delhi, Delhi 110048, India

name: Whose Art Is It Anyway?
description: Who gets to create, define, and own art?About the LectureArt is often spoken about as something universal, but in reality, art has never existed outside systems of access.This participatory lecture explores how artists from historically marginalised communities navigate spaces where their work is frequently appropriated, diluted, celebrated without context, or dismissed altogether. Drawing from performance and narrative traditions across South Asia, the session asks what shapes artistic legitimacy — and who gets excluded from it.The lecture explores:How caste, class, gender, religion, and sexuality influence who is allowed to create and whose work is valuedWhy certain forms of expression become “mainstream,” while others are labelled “folk,” “obscene,” or “political”How art can function both as a site of erasure and a tool of assertionWhat it means to witness, consume, and engage with art responsiblyThrough examples, discussion, and interactive prompts, participants will reflect on the relationship between art, memory, dignity, and justice.At its core, this lecture invites us to see art not as passive representation, but as a way of negotiating identity, resistance, and belonging.About the SpeakerSri Vamsi Matta is a Bangalore-based interdisciplinary theatre artist whose work is rooted in lived experience, political memory, and anti-caste thought. Born into a Dalit community, his practice engages with everyday and structural violence of caste, positioning art as a space for refusal, repair, and collective imagination.Working across theatre, performance, writing, and participatory formats, his work brings together autobiography, community history, and radical pedagogy. His recent works include Star in the Sky, exploring the emotional and political lives of Dalit students in Indian universities, and Come Eat With Me, a participatory performance examining caste through food and shared meals, staged across India, the United States, and Australia.Vamsi has facilitated workshops, lectures, and teaching engagements across institutions including Azim Premji University, Ashoka University, Northwestern University, and the University of Texas at Austin, bringing anti-caste artistic practice into conversation with diverse audiences nationally and internationally.All Grounds for Thought tickets include a ₹250 coupon redeemable on food and beverages at the venue.About unLectureunLecture brings experts out of classrooms and into cafés, bars and neighbourhood spaces across Delhi. Created by three friends from college who wanted learning to feel warm, social and alive. unLecture turns an otherwise ordinary evening into a conversation you may think about long after it ends.

name: The Blue Moon Sound Bath
description: A rare celestial event. A shared energy. A real shift. The Immersion Room in collaboration with Eka by Paayal invites you into an immersive Blue Moon Sound Bath — a deeply calming evening designed to help you slow down, release emotional heaviness, and reconnect with yourself. Inspired by the symbolic energy of the Blue Moon, this experience becomes a gentle space for reflection, renewal, and inner stillness.As you arrive, you’ll be guided through grounding breathwork, intention setting, and a soothing meditation before sinking into a live sound immersion using healing instruments like sound bowls and chimes. The vibrations create a deeply restorative atmosphere that many describe as calming, expansive, and emotionally releasing.This is not just a meditation session — it is a collective pause from the noise of everyday life. A space where emotions soften, the mind quiets, and the body enters deep relaxation. Whether you are completely new to sound healing or already familiar with wellness practices, the experience is designed to feel accessible, nurturing, and immersive.Come as you are. Leave feeling lighter.

name: The Tiny Hands Art Camp - 10 Days with 10 Mediums
description: Give your child two weeks away from screens and into a space filled with colour, curiosity, imagination, and hands-on making. ✨At Between Easels, children are introduced to 10 different art mediums through playful, guided sessions designed to help them explore freely, think creatively, and build confidence through art. From painting and printmaking to sculpture, collage, and clay — every class becomes a new creative adventure.Led by practicing artists in a warm and encouraging studio environment, the workshop focuses not on perfection, but on expression, observation, experimentation, and joy. 🎨The program concludes with a special exhibition where children proudly display their artworks for friends and family — celebrating everything they’ve created over the two weeks. 🖼️BETWEEN EASELS — KIDS WORKSHOP SCHEDULE10 Days • 10 Mediums • Endless Creativity⏰ Class Timing: 10 AM – 12 PM🎨 Suitable for all skill levels, ages 6+• 1 June (Mon) — Abstract ArtExplore colours, textures, movement, and playful mark-making through expressive painting exercises and creative experimentation.• 2 June (Tue) — Still Life DrawingLearn observation, light, shadows, and basic geometric forms through pencil and charcoal sketching.• 3 June (Wed) — CollagingCreate layered artworks using paper cut-outs, textures, found materials, and imaginative composition techniques.• 4 June (Thu) — Paint Like PicassoExperiment with bold colours, playful portraits, abstract faces, and cubist-inspired visual storytelling.• 5 June (Fri) — MonoprintingDiscover simple printmaking methods to create expressive, one-of-a-kind textured prints.✨ Weekend Off• 8 June (Mon) — Collective Painting & CollageWork together on large collaborative artworks using paint, collage, textures, and mixed media.• 9 June (Tue) — 3D POP SculptureBuild playful three-dimensional forms through sculptural making, construction, and hands-on material exploration.• 10 June (Wed) — Block PrintingDesign and print repeating patterns using carved blocks, shapes, textures, and vibrant inks.• 11 June (Thu) — CollographyExplore texture-based printmaking by layering materials to create embossed and experimental surfaces.• 12 June (Fri) — Clay PortraitsSculpt expressive clay portraits while learning facial structure, textures, and semi-relief techniques.✨ Weekend OffFor registrations & enquiries:📞 Ramansh — 9818398552📞 Mansha — 9971822458

name: Tuesday Board Game Night
description: Tuesday Game Nights started in 2017 in Ahmedabad to bring people together by unlocking the kid inside. It worked. Strangers became friends. Friends became family. We are now bringing it to Delhi. This Tuesday, skip the screens. Bring your dad for Codenames. Bring your kid for Azul. Most importantly bring yourself for the laughter, you didn’t know you missed. 📍Project Otenga, Shaheedi Park, ITO | 7 PM onwards All the games will be taught during the evening by our expert game facilitator, Sharadha Jain, and there is no requirement to know the games before.Please call / whatsapp us on 9910203373 for group discounts.The coupons for tickets are redeemable.

name: The Invisible World Around Us
description: Join us for a lecture-demonstration that reveals the hidden systems quietly shaping everyday life, the signals, networks, and invisible conversations happening all around us, all the time.About the LectureModern life feels seamless.Your phone connects instantly. Devices speak to one another invisibly. Signals move through walls, across cities, and between bodies and machines without us ever seeing them.But beneath that apparent simplicity lies an invisible world of constant activity.This session is an interactive exploration of the hidden technological layer surrounding us every day, from wireless communication systems to biological signals generated within our own bodies.Through live demonstrations and visualisations, the lecture reveals the silent digital atmosphere we move through constantly, exposing how devices communicate, how networks remain connected, and how fragile these invisible systems can actually be.The session explores:The unseen wireless systems constantly operating around usHow digital communication networks function — and how easily they can be disruptedWhat invisible technological infrastructures modern life depends onHow biological signals inside the human body can be translated into machine movementWhat human-machine interaction might look like in the futureThe lecture also includes demonstrations involving EMG-controlled interfaces and experimental hardware systems that make invisible interactions tangible and visible in real time.About the SpeakerRahul Thareja is a hardware hacker and robotics engineer whose work explores the invisible systems shaping modern technological life.Working across experimental hardware, wireless system visualisation, and human-machine interfaces, his practice focuses on making hidden technological interactions visible, understandable, and open to questioning.With a background in robotics engineering and education, Rahul combines technical experimentation with a curiosity-driven approach to public engagement, creating interactive experiences that make complex systems tangible for broader audiences.His work sits at the intersection of exploration, awareness, and interaction — encouraging people to see familiar technologies in entirely unfamiliar ways.Your Ticket Includes a 300/- Coupon Redeemable on F&BAbout unLectureunLecture brings experts out of classrooms and into cafés, bars and neighbourhood spaces across Delhi. Created by three friends from college who wanted learning to feel warm, social and alive. unLecture turns an otherwise ordinary evening into a conversation you may think about long after it ends.

name: Tiffin Trails: Toontooni’s Table x Swarnavo Datta
description: Come enjoy an afternoon of local cuisines across India as Swarnavo and Toonika take you on a culinary journey. Inspired by Swarnavo's book, The Great Indian Tiffin Box, this is a journey you don't want to miss. Share a table with strangers who will soon become friends and break bread together.

name: Chinese Whispers: India and China Before 1962
description: Join us for a lecture that revisits one of the most consequential relationships in modern Asian history, not through the lens of war, but through the fragile optimism that came before it.About the LectureToday, India and China are often discussed as rivals.But there was a moment when many believed the two newly independent civilizations would together shape the future of Asia and the postcolonial world.At the centre of that story stood K.M. Panikkar — historian, diplomat, strategist, and India’s ambassador to China during the tumultuous years following Mao’s revolution.What did Panikkar actually see when he arrived in Beijing after 1949? Why did he believe Communist China could be accommodated within an Asian framework rather than treated as an existential threat? And how did the promise of solidarity gradually give way to suspicion, miscalculation, and conflict?This lecture explores one of the most enduring debates in Indian foreign policy.The session examines:The early relationship between India and the newly established People's Republic of ChinaK.M. Panikkar’s role in shaping India's understanding of ChinaThe political and diplomatic tensions surrounding TibetHow the vision of Asian solidarity emerged — and why it ultimately fracturedWhether Panikkar misread China, or was unfairly blamed for a much larger strategic failureDrawing on archival research, diplomatic history, and political biography, the lecture revisits a period when the future of Asia appeared open, uncertain, and full of possibility.About the SpeakerNarayani Basu is a historian, foreign policy analyst, and award-winning author whose work focuses on the lesser-known individuals who shaped modern India.She is the bestselling author of V.P. Menon: The Unsung Architect of Modern India and Allegiance: Azaadi & the End of Empire, both widely praised for bringing overlooked figures and perspectives into the story of Indian independence.Her latest book, A Man for All Seasons, a biography of K.M. Panikkar, has received widespread critical acclaim and was awarded the AutHer Award for Best Non-Fiction Book in 2025.Through her writing and research, Narayani brings together biography, diplomacy, and political history to illuminate the personalities and decisions that continue to shape India's place in the world.Your Ticket Includes a 300/- Coupon Redeemable on F&BAbout unLectureunLecture brings experts out of classrooms and into cafés, bars and neighbourhood spaces across Delhi. Created by three friends from college who wanted learning to feel warm, social and alive. unLecture turns an otherwise ordinary evening into a conversation you may think about long after it ends.

location.address: E-17, Market Ln, Hauz Khas Market, Block E, Hauz Khas, Delhi, New Delhi, Delhi 110016, India

name: The Good, the Bad, & the Ugly Sun
description: About the LectureThe Sun feels constant.Every morning it rises. It warms the planet. It makes life on Earth possible.But beneath that apparent stability lies a star in perpetual turbulence, a seething sphere of plasma and magnetic fields capable of eruptions so powerful they can disrupt satellites, collapse communication systems, and interfere with power grids across the planet.This lecture begins with one of the greatest mysteries in modern astrophysics: Why is the Sun’s outer atmosphere, the corona, hundreds of times hotter than its visible surface?It’s a paradox that seems to defy common sense, like walking away from a fire and somehow feeling warmer.From there, the session journeys into the hidden physics of the Sun: solar flares, coronal mass ejections, magnetic storms, and the invisible streams of charged particles constantly travelling toward Earth. The lecture revisits the famous Carrington Event of 1859 — the most intense solar storm ever recorded — when telegraph systems burst into sparks and auroras lit skies far beyond the poles.The session explores:Why the Sun is far more dynamic and unstable than it appearsHow solar eruptions shape “space weather” around EarthWhat happens when solar storms collide with modern technological systemsHow missions like India’s Aditya-L1 are helping scientists study and predict solar activityWhy understanding the Sun has become essential in a technology-dependent worldBlending astronomy, physics, history, and storytelling, this lecture invites us to see the Sun not as a distant backdrop to life on Earth — but as a powerful cosmic force we remain deeply dependent on, and vulnerable to.About the SpeakerProf. Vaibhav Pant is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Physics at IIT Delhi and a solar physicist whose work focuses on understanding the Sun and its impact on Earth.He completed his PhD from the Indian Institute of Astrophysics, Bangalore, followed by postdoctoral research at KU Leuven in Belgium and the Instituto de Astrofísica de Canarias in Spain. Before joining IIT Delhi, he served as Scientist at ARIES, Nainital for five years.His research explores waves in the solar atmosphere, large-scale solar eruptions, and space weather phenomena. He has published over 50 refereed research papers and has been closely associated with India’s Aditya-L1 solar mission, serving as Principal Investigator of the Aditya-L1 Support Cell and organizing multiple national workshops under the programme.Beyond research, he is deeply invested in science communication and has delivered invited talks across major international conferences in Asia and Europe. Outside physics, he enjoys reading non-fiction, playing table tennis, and watching thoughtful films and Korean dramas.About unLectureunLecture brings experts out of classrooms and into cafés, bars and neighbourhood spaces across Delhi. Created by three friends from college who wanted learning to feel warm, social and alive. unLecture turns an otherwise ordinary evening into a conversation you may think about long after it ends.

name: Aam Nama
description: Aam Nama with Sohail Hashmi is a mango-centric cultural experience celebrating the stories, recipes, and traditions of Old Delhi.The session will be led by renowned historian Sohail Hashmi, who will take guests through the rich history of mangoes, their connection with Delhi’s food culture, and the memories and traditions that surround them. Through engaging storytelling, lesser-known anecdotes, and cultural insights, the session will explore how mangoes have been cherished across generations.The experience will also include heirloom recipes from Old Delhi kitchens, a pocket recipe book launch, and a specially curated mango tasting—bringing together history, flavour, nostalgia and community in one memorable evening at Kathika Cultural Centre, Old Delhi.

location.address: 1237, Gali Khatikan, Imli Mohalla, Mohalla IMLI, Kucha Pati Ram, Sitaram Bazar, Delhi, 110006, India

name: The Therapeutic Art of  Wood Whittling
description: Step into the calming world of carving with The Therapeutic Art of Wood Whittling — a first-of-its-kind hands-on whittling experience in Delhi NCR, hosted at our studio by the incredibly talented artist Viney Anand, who brings over 5 years of wood whittling experience to the craft.Designed for beginners and creative enthusiasts alike, this immersive workshop is all about slowing down, working with your hands, and experiencing the quiet satisfaction of transforming a simple piece of wood into something meaningful. Under Viney’s guidance, you’ll learn the fundamentals of safe wood whittling, carving techniques, tool handling, and finishing methods in a warm and welcoming environment.All tools, safety equipment, and materials are fully included, so you can simply arrive and enjoy the process. By the end of the session, you’ll take home your very own handcrafted wooden project along with a deeply therapeutic and memorable experience.Whether you’re looking to unwind, explore a new skill, reconnect with creativity, or simply experience the joy of slow craftsmanship, this is a workshop you wouldn’t want to miss.

location.address: 2M, 33, Shahid Bhagat Singh Marg, Block M, New Industrial Twp 2, New Industrial Township, Faridabad, Haryana 121001, India

name: ALT EFF Film Club x GuruJal Gurugram | 19th June
description: Join us for a screening of Miles Away; a visual and auditory immersion of the lives of migrant, female brick labors. This film is intimate, cinematic and sensory.Part of our 2024 Program, this was Juror Dia Mirza's reaction:"I was very moved by 'Miles Away' as for the first time I saw the perspective of what Climate Change is doing to women. It was very special to see so many women coming together to tell the story and feeling at ease as they shared personal moments with the female filmmakers. I felt as if I was a part of their lives."To become a Film Club Member, continue with the regular booking process. The membership option will appear after you select the venue and date.All activities, including discussions and refreshments, will occur between 7:00 and 9:00 PM.What's Included?Exclusive screenings of the world's best environmental cinemaAccess to a community of environmentally inclined, film-loving peoplePost-film discussions.Student Discount50% off for students with a valid ID (& must bring ID to the screening).CODE: ALTSTUDENTABOUT ALT EFFIf you are tired of the outrage cycle, doomscrolling collapse, and the looming AI takeover narrative. If you have caught yourself going numb just to cope. We see you.All Living Things Environmental Film Festival is back with the third season of Film Club. A monthly meet up across 12 cities over 5 months, orbiting environmental cinema that cracks you open without trapping you in doom and gloom.It is a room full of people choosing to show up in cities whose infrastructure persuades you to stay home, choosing to sit together, watch together, and turn private anxiety into collective imagination. Messy. Human. Honest conversation. With snacks. Always snacks.

name: Sex, Death, And The Long War Of Life
description: Join us for a lecture that begins with a provocative proposition:You were never meant to exist.And that may be the most liberating fact about you.About the LectureHuman beings like stories.We tell ourselves that life has meaning, that history bends toward progress, that our desires are uniquely our own.Evolution tells a different story.One without heroes, villains, purpose, or justice.This lecture explores the oldest conflict on Earth: the endless struggle of living things to survive, reproduce, and persist. Long before nations, religions, or political ideologies existed, life was already engaged in a ruthless competition that continues to shape every organism alive today.Drawing on evolutionary biology and psychology, the session traces how some of our deepest instincts emerged from this ancient inheritance.The lecture explores:Why evolution remains the most powerful framework for understanding lifeHow competition and cooperation coexist in the natural worldWhat sex is really doing from the perspective of evolutionWhy human beings are, like all organisms, unfinished creaturesHow morality, consciousness, and even the idea of a “soul” might be viewed through an evolutionary lensMoving from algae to human behaviour, from reproduction to mortality, the session challenges many of the comforting assumptions we hold about ourselves.At its heart lies a difficult but strangely freeing question:What changes when we stop imagining that life was designed for us?About the SpeakerAmbarish Satwik is a Delhi-based vascular and endovascular surgeon, Director of the Vascular Cath Lab at Sir Ganga Ram Hospital, and Professor of Vascular Surgery at GRIPMER.Alongside his medical career, he is an acclaimed writer whose work moves across literature, history, politics, and science. His novel Perineum: Nether Parts of the Empire is a critically acclaimed exploration of sexuality and power in the British Raj.His essays, columns, and commentaries have appeared in publications including India Today, The Times of India, Outlook, Open, Tehelka, Time Out, and Hindu Business Line.Known for bringing together scientific rigour, intellectual irreverence, and dark humour, his talks often leave audiences questioning ideas they previously took for granted.Ticket IncludesYour ticket includes a complimentary drink during the session.About unLectureunLecture brings experts out of classrooms and into cafés, bars and neighbourhood spaces across Delhi. Created by three friends from college who wanted learning to feel warm, social and alive. unLecture turns an otherwise ordinary evening into a conversation you may think about long after it ends.
